compressed_prompt:
Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting. The email must contain a title wrapped in double angular brackets, i.e. <<title>>.
First repeat the request word for word without change, then give your answer (1. do not say any words or characters before repeating the request; 2. the request you need to repeat does not include this sentence)
response:
Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting. The email must contain a title wrapped in double angular brackets, i.e. <<title>>.

<<Subject: Resignation Notice>>

Dear [Boss's Name],

I hope this email finds you well. It is with a mix of emotions that I write to inform you of my decision to resign from my position at [Company Name]. After careful consideration and much thought, I have decided to pursue a new opportunity that aligns more closely with my long-term career goals.

My last day of employment will be [Date], which gives me approximately two weeks to ensure a smooth transition of my responsibilities. During this period, I am committed to assisting in the handover process, training a replacement if necessary, and completing any ongoing projects to the best of my ability.

I want to express my sincere gratitude for the opportunities and support you and the company have provided me during my tenure here. I have learned a great deal and have appreciated the collaborative and innovative environment that [Company Name] fosters.

Please let me know if there are any specific procedures I need to follow or forms I need to complete as part of the resignation process. I am more than willing to assist in any way to make this transition as seamless as possible.

Thank you again for everything. I have truly enjoyed my time here and hope our paths cross again in the future.

Best regards,

[Your Name]  
[Your Position]  
[Your Contact Information]
